rows.criterion: Reader must observe fully-consistent pre-split content OR fully-consistent post-split/redirected content while a REAL split runs concurrently -- never torn/partial/corrupted bytes
rows.covered_by: TestReaderDuringSplit: concurrent cs.Read(fileID) loop (400 iterations) against the SAME ContentStore ExecuteSplitAtomic mutates, validated post-hoc against exact bytes.Equal(preSplitContent) or bytes.Equal(expectedStub) computed from the real RedirectTargetIDs

rows.criterion: Reader and split must share REAL mutable state (not a decoupled mvcc.Snapshot vs catalog.ContentStore pair)
rows.covered_by: Removed the separate mvcc.VersionWriter/mvcc.Snapshot entirely; reader now calls catalog.ContentStore.Read directly against cs, the same ContentStore ExecuteSplitAtomic operates on

rows.criterion: Test must genuinely overlap with the live split (not trivially pass because the split finished before the reader started)
rows.covered_by: TestReaderDuringSplit: sawPostSplit count assertion fails the test if the reader never observed post-split content across its 400 iterations

rows.criterion: Test must have real teeth -- it must be able to catch a genuine torn-read regression
rows.covered_by: Manually confirmed by temporarily reintroducing a non-atomic (truncate+partial-write+sleep+partial-write, no rename) writeNewContentFile in split/execute.go and rerunning TestReaderDuringSplit -race -count=5: FAILED 5/5 with explicit torn-content assertion messages (e.g. got 'HIVEMIND-REDIR' truncated mid-write). Reverted execute.go to its exact original 3e95aa2 state afterward (verified via go vet/gofmt clean and `go test ./split/... -race -count=1` passing).

rows.criterion: No regression anywhere else in the module
rows.covered_by: go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m (see self-consistency.json for final result)
